description: A nylon pouch for storing 40mm grenades.  Could also be used to store other types of grenades, like 37mm, flashbangs, smoke, and so on.

variants.description: An open-top nylon pouch for storing 40mm grenades.  Could also be used to store other types of grenades, like 37mm, flashbangs, smoke, and so on.  Like many other pouches, this one flattens out when not filled.  <variant:multicam>

An open-top nylon pouch for storing 40mm grenades.  Could also be used to store other types of grenades, like 37mm, flashbangs, smoke, and so on.  Like many other pouches, this one flattens out when not filled.  <variant:black>

description: A small nylon pouch for storing a single hand grenade.  This model is quick to operate because it uses a hook-and-loop flap instead of a plastic buckle.

variants.description: A small nylon pouch, intended for storing a single standard-issue hand grenade.  Can also fit a single grenade of slightly-larger volume.  Unlike most models on the market, this one is relatively quick to operate, because it uses a hook-and-loop strap, rather than a plastic buckle.  There's a thin thread outline of an M67 grenade on the flap, and a print that says "FRAG OUT!" on the opposite side of it.  <variant:multicam>

description: A pair of nylon grenade pouches sewn together.  Slightly slower to operate than the alternatives.

variants.description: A pair of small nylon bags sewn together, each holding a single standard-issue hand grenade.  Because each pouch uses a plastic buckle for holding itself closed, they're relatively slow to operate.  The pair is slightly more compact than using two separate pouches.  <variant:multicam>

description: A nylon pouch for storing a flashbang grenade.  It can also accomodate a smoke grenade.

variants.description: A nylon pouch for storing a single flashbang grenade.  It is large enough to fit a smoke grenade as well.  <variant:multicam>

description: A nylon pouch for storing up to six 40mm grenades.  Could also be used to store other types of grenades, like 37mm, flashbangs, smoke, and so on.
